# Description
This implements a world to data transformation for normals, which is needed by the render plane implementation.

#7417 fixed #7409, but did not add a test for the buggy behaviour. Here's a sketch of how a test could work:

- make a viewer
- add a viewer callback for [Return], that simply counts the number of calls
- find the layer list widget in the Qt window
- use the method to set the text editing field focus (I don't know how to do this but I'm sure there's a function somewhere 😅)
- use qtbot to press the relevant keys, say N, E, W, [space], N, A, M, E, [return]
- check that the layer name has been appropriately updated
- check that the viewer callback has not been called","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/7423/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/7423/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

Plan","user":{"login":"DragaDoncila","id":17995243,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE3OTk1MjQz","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/17995243?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/DragaDoncila","html_url":"https://github.com/DragaDoncila","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DragaDoncila/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DragaDoncila/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DragaDoncila/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DragaDoncila/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DragaDoncila/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DragaDoncila/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DragaDoncila/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DragaDoncila/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DragaDoncila/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":3028315226,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwzMDI4MzE1MjI2","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/labels/task","name":"task","color":"1D76DB","default":false,"description":"Tasks maintainers"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":19,"created_at":"2024-06-21T05:10:59Z","updated_at":"2024-12-02T08:41:33Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"## 🧰 Task
This issue is here to track current state of implementation w.r.t NAP-6. 

### Other Related Considerations
- [x] npe2 PR adding a mapping from command IDs to associated menu items for each plugin
    - just needs review I think?
    - useful because we need both the command object and the menu contrib object without iterating
- [x] PR adding empty placeholders to menus
    - If you look at #7011 you'll notice the empty submenus are not great as a UI experience. #6950 was the first attempt to address this issue, but it was ultimately implemented in #7038
    - [ ] The code needs testing and the keys should be moved off the layerlist context
- [ ] On contexts, keys, and context hierarchies


### Core NAP-6

- [x] Add `Layers -> Layer Type` and `Layers -> Data` submenus for conversions
    - not only can we populate with our own stuff, but @jni and I found stuff that could go in there from Tools menu
    - [x] update NAP
- [x] bring out stuff from `Layers -> Edit` into top `Layers`
    - Peter, Juan and I spoke about this at North America meeting and we felt that having top `Edit` and  `Layers -> Edit` is weird, but if we just move them out we can avoid this issue.
    - [x] NAP
- [x] top level Layers not contributable
    - [x] NAP
    - [x] maybe need to update the bit about submenu
- [x] name of plugin should be shown in menu next to action name
    - [x] pure commands need plugin name

This MVP version of NAP-6 will be released in v0.5.0.

- [ ] Add top level edit menu
    - [ ] update NAP
- [ ] Distribute layer context actions among Edit and Layers menus
    - [ ] update NAP
- [ ] Add top-level Develop menu (replaces Debug menu)
	- [ ] Activated by a preference
	- [ ] update NAP 
- [ ] Order & grouping of items
    - [ ] napari always on top
    - [ ] plugin declared group and order respected 
- [ ]  Decide exactly what types may be used as type hints for dependency injection in a plugin command function (these are the names declared in the `app.injection_store.namespace`), and ensure we have providers for these types. Also document this.
    - [ ] We are in rough agreement that "pure" commands in the `Layers` menu should work on the selected layer (if they take a layer at all). This should be documented and made explicit in the code.
- [ ] Decide on which context keys we want to make available to plugin developers for use in `when` and `enablement` expressions, where we expose them, and how developers can add more.
    - [ ] Implement parsing and respecting `when` expressions for menu items
    - [ ] Implement parsing and respecting `enablement` expressions for commands
- [ ] Allow plugins to declare arbitrary submenus under their own plugin’s `self` menu
    - [ ] NAP update from plugin name to `self`
- [ ] Add icon/something to signal when a menu item is a widget, so users will know when clicking on something will open a widget vs run something (we can and potentially should ensure adherence on the napari side)
- [ ] Adding name of plugin to menu item titles
    - [ ] @jni thinks we should be stripping `napari` from the plugin name (even the display name??) but I have concerns about mangling plugin names 
    - [ ] currently the name of the plugin also appears when the action is shown in the `Plugins` menu, even though all actions are grouped under a submenu of the same name. This is visually quite annoying, so we should look at whether we can strip the plugin name in this case. Note that a single action can't have different titles depending on the menu it's part of, so we need to either implement this, or change the name of the qt object directly.
- [ ] A key goal for our menu contributions is that users are never surprised by the outcome of clicking on an action. Since actions can come from all sorts of sources and **may** have destructive effects, we want to provide as much information as possible to users about each menu item
    - [ ] Add `description` field to manifest - make it required??
        - [ ] does it live on the `command` or on the `MenuContribution`? 
    - [ ] Read `description` into `status tip` field of `Action`
        - [ ] is that a super weird place to put it? Will users ever look there? 
- [ ] Rather than using the full menu path for contributable menus, plugin developers should be able to specify a unique key, and we complete the full menu path for them
    - [ ] Decide on the unique keys
    - [ ] Implement unravelling
    - [ ] Backwards compatibility? 
    - [ ] NAP
- [ ] Icons
    - [ ] Look at whether icons are an option for disambiguating menu item source (rather than full name) 
    - [ ] Figure out how to use superqt.fonticons and whether this should be exposed to plugin devs
    - [ ] Do they work in menu bars?
    - [ ] Add docs and potential examples to plugin template
- [ ] sort plugins menu alphabetically (lifted from #6410 as we should be doing this anyway really) 
- [ ] test coverage

### Next Gen Manifest
- [ ] Decide on schema for next gen manifest
    - [ ] Address concerns in #6227
- [ ] Formalize which `Action` fields are exposed to users via the manifest, and which contributions they are exposed in. Related issue #6224
- [ ] Update iterator APIs to match the new manifest structure and how we iterate in `napari` when registering actions
    - [ ] Related issue #6326  

### Doc Updates

- [ ] everything from implementation plan
- [ ] pure commands in Layers should act on selected layer
- [ ] more detail on each discussion/decision in bottom summary
- [ ] …s for widgets not …s for commands
- [x]  open issue add todo list point from nap
- [ ] context menu?
	- now that we've removed it from contributables, need to maybe update first example or say something about removal
- [ ] add usage documentation
- [ ] document exported context keys
- [ ] expose MenuContribution guide in npe2
- [ ] add menu contributions to napari-plugin-template

References and relevant issues
Closes #7415 

# Description
Add metadata event in Layer EmitterGroup

### Example

```python
from skimage import data

import napari

# create the viewer with an image
viewer = napari.view_image(data.astronaut(), rgb=True)

def observer(event):
    print(event.source.metadata)

viewer.layers[0].events.metadata.connect(observer)

viewer.layers[0].metadata = {'name': 'astronaut'} # prints {'name': 'astronaut'}
viewer.layers[0].metadata['name'] = 'astro' # does not print anything

if __name__ == '__main__':
    napari.run()
```

Note that the setter is not triggered when you manipulate directly the underlying dictionary object. Closes #7415 

# Description
Add metadata event in Layer EmitterGroup

### Example

```python
from skimage import data

import napari

# create the viewer with an image
viewer = napari.view_image(data.astronaut(), rgb=True)

def observer(event):
    print(event.source.metadata)

viewer.layers[0].events.metadata.connect(observer)

viewer.layers[0].metadata = {'name': 'astronaut'} # prints {'name': 'astronaut'}
viewer.layers[0].metadata['name'] = 'astro' # does not print anything

if __name__ == '__main__':
    napari.run()
```

Note that the setter is not triggered when you manipulate directly the underlying dictionary object. !

## 🚀 Feature
Add metadata event in Layer EmitterGroup

## Motivation

For a plugin I would like to check when the layer's metadata is changed. For that I think the simple way is to user `my_layer.events.metadata`. Maybe there is a reason it is not already the case that I didn't understand?

## Possible implementation

Adding an event here: https://github.com/napari/napari/blob/8ba709156721b9a7050b282114379963c94dc7ad/napari/layers/base/base.py#L455 called `metadata`

And using metadata setter to add `self.events.metadata()`
https://github.com/napari/napari/blob/8ba709156721b9a7050b282114379963c94dc7ad/napari/layers/base/base.py#L669-L672

Thank you!

## 🚀 Feature
Add metadata event in Layer EmitterGroup

## Motivation

For a plugin I would like to check when the layer's metadata is changed. For that I think the simple way is to user `my_layer.events.metadata`. Maybe there is a reason it is not already the case that I didn't understand?

## Possible implementation

Adding an event here: https://github.com/napari/napari/blob/8ba709156721b9a7050b282114379963c94dc7ad/napari/layers/base/base.py#L455 called `metadata`

And using metadata setter to add `self.events.metadata()`
https://github.com/napari/napari/blob/8ba709156721b9a7050b282114379963c94dc7ad/napari/layers/base/base.py#L669-L672

Thank you!

{"repository_id":144513571,"push_id":21493267370,"size":1,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/main","head":"528bb54b85c217b80b34d92389315538817eaec5","before":"73053c6ce474c9865a451a0eaf526441bd54b66d","commits":[{"sha":"528bb54b85c217b80b34d92389315538817eaec5","author":{"email":"48f656bb160d363035fd3aee9bd75aa937c5c4d9@users.noreply.github.com","name":"Clement Caporal"},"message":"Add metadata event to Layer class (#7416)

# References and relevant issues
Closes #7415 

# Description
Add metadata event in Layer EmitterGroup

### Example

```python
from skimage import data

import napari

# create the viewer with an image
viewer = napari.view_image(data.astronaut(), rgb=True)

def observer(event):
    print(event.source.metadata)

viewer.layers[0].events.metadata.connect(observer)

viewer.layers[0].metadata = {'name': 'astronaut'} # prints {'name': 'astronaut'}
viewer.layers[0].metadata['name'] = 'astro' # does not print anything

if __name__ == '__main__':
    napari.run()
```

Note that the setter is not triggered when you manipulate directly the
underlying dictionary object. I don't know if this is something we want?","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/commits/528bb54b85c217b80b34d92389315538817eaec5"}]}

# References and relevant issues

Partially addresses #7382

# Description
This PR marks the 10 slowest tests on my local machine. This allows me
to include or exclude when I run tests locally.

This could be an initial proof of concept step that could be used to
identify some slow
tests on CI runs and treat them differently than the rest of the test
suite. Perhaps exclude on some test matrix runs or parallelize the slow
tests.

🐛 Bug Report

Shared University machine had Napari installed by an Admin but users could not run.
Turns out they require Write access to the Napari install folder (C://Program Files/Python/Lib/site-packages/napari/utils/colormaps/.... seems to be the particular folder that it hangs on) for Napari to launch.

This is a bit of a pain for sysadmins deploying Napari.


### 💡 Steps to Reproduce

1. Install Napari as an administrator
2. Attempt to run Napari without write perms on the Napari folder.
3. Napari fails to launch but does not give an error.

### 💡 Expected Behavior

Napari will launch.

### 🌎 Environment

napari: 0.5.3
Platform: Windows-10-10.0.22631-SP0
Python: 3.11.9 (tags/v3.11.9:de54cf5, Apr  2 2024, 10:12:12) [MSC v.1938 64 bit (AMD64)]
Qt: 5.15.2
PyQt5: 5.15.11
NumPy: 2.0.2

Dask: 2024.9.0
VisPy: 0.14.3
magicgui: 0.9.1
superqt: 0.6.7
in-n-out: 0.2.1
app-model: 0.2.8
npe2: 0.7.7
 
OpenGL:
  - GL version:  4.6.0 - Build 31.0.101.4502
  - MAX_TEXTURE_SIZE: 16384
  - GL_MAX_3D_TEXTURE_SIZE: 2048
 
Screens:
  - screen 1: resolution 3840x2160, scale 1.0
 
Optional:
  - numba: 0.60.0
  - triangle: 20230923
  - napari-plugin-manager: 0.1.1
 
Settings path:
  - C:\Users\pa459\AppData\Local\napari\Python311_b4a7f09a69f8de457c9fc9104726cbfe1219b71d\settings.yaml
Plugins:
  - cavendish-particle-tracks: 0.1.0b1 (2 contributions)

When switching to 3D view in async, the viewer displays the already-loaded 2D array in 3D space before replacing the data with a real 3D node, asynchronously. Before the full 3D texture is loaded, and since #7126, hovering the mouse over the canvas triggers get_value, which gets evaluated to _get_value_3d, which fails because the array is 2D at this moment.


**Edit:** link to the traceback 😅: https://gist.github.com/jni/a244d3931f8eadd8fc7e64887feff2ca

Summary:

```pytb
File ~/projects/napari/napari/components/viewer_model.py:571, in ViewerModel._calc_status_from_cursor
--> 571     status = active.get_status(
File ~/projects/napari/napari/layers/base/base.py:2142, in Layer.get_status
-> 2142     value = self.get_value(
File ~/projects/napari/napari/layers/base/base.py:1404, in Layer.get_value
-> 1404         value = self._get_value_3d(
File ~/projects/napari/napari/layers/_scalar_field/scalar_field.py:687, in ScalarFieldBase._get_value_3d
--> 687     return self._get_value_ray(
File ~/projects/napari/napari/layers/_scalar_field/scalar_field.py:655, in ScalarFieldBase._get_value_ray
--> 655     values = im_slice[tuple(clamped.T)]
IndexError: too many indices for array: array is 2-dimensional, but 3 were indexed
```

### 💡 Steps to Reproduce

1. run `NAPARI_ASYNC=1 napari https://uk1s3.embassy.ebi.ac.uk/idr/zarr/v0.4/idr0001A/2551.zarr/A/1/0`
2. select a visible image layer
3. switch to 3D mode
4. hover the mouse over the canvas

### 💡 Expected Behavior

No error should be raised.

### 🌎 Environment

```
napari: 0.5.4rc4.dev0+gc73e9d5ec.d20241014
Platform: macOS-14.5-arm64-arm-64bit

OpenGL:
  - GL version:  2.1 Metal - 88.1
  - MAX_TEXTURE_SIZE: 16384
  - GL_MAX_3D_TEXTURE_SIZE: 2048

Screens:
  - screen 1: resolution 1800x1169, scale 2.0

Optional:
  - numba: 0.60.0
  - triangle not installed
  - napari-plugin-manager: 0.1.3

Settings path:

# Description

Change `mode` following `visible` attribute value (changing `mode` to be pan/zoom if the layer is not visible and saving the previous mode to restore it when the layer is visible again) and prevent changing `mode` to something different to pan/zoom while the layer is not visible:

References and relevant issues

Addresses / Closes? #7425 

# Description

Examples using `screenshot` and `figure_export` were found to have a grayscale canvas in the docs. The grayscale appears to be from the `flash=True` argument, so this PR sets `flash=False` to fix the display of the viewer for the Gallery. The canvas will now properly be color. 

This does not fix the root cause of the grayscale canvas as a result of `flash=True`.

Tested with a local docs build (finally!)

Thank you @psobolewskiPhD for assistance.

References and relevant issues
Followup on https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/6849#issuecomment-2095676630

# Description
The idea is to have our own custom handler for log messages so we can then expose the logs as we prefer from the GUI. This will make it easier for users to copy-paste logs even if they didn't manually open from the console, and also easier for developers to quickly check things.

I never really worked with the logging module, so I have a feeling that this is garbage... but oh well, let's see how bad :P

To test, open napari and do some stuff. Then, from the terminal or console run:
```py
napari._LOG_STREAM.logs_at_level(10)
```

EDIT: Added a very quick-and-dirty GUI for the logger based on the implementation of the `About` widget. For how bad it is, it's actually ok xD You can open it from the menu `Help -> Show log".

cc @czaki

References and relevant issues
closes #7080 

# Description
Currently napari has the capability to add a path to the shapes layer, but it does not allow for adding a more freehand drawing of a line in the shapes layer. One could do this using the labels layer, but the downside of that is that the size of the label layer being created is at least equal to the current extent of layer data in the viewer. This PR adds the capability of adding a polyline to the shapes layer, very much like freehand drawing of a line. This functionality works both by using the mouse or a pencil on a tablet.

Early feedback is welcome, but just of note this PR contains some obvious things that would be improved. However, this PR serves also for a video on how to contribute to napari which would include a part on reviewing process for which some obvious things that could be improved are added.

This work is the result of a napari code cafe.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/7099/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/7099/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/comments/2513860551","html_url":"https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/7099#issuecomment-2513860551","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/7099","id":2513860551,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCJ0aI86V1nfH","user":{"login":"jni","id":492549,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ5MjU0OQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/492549?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni","html_url":"https://github.com/jni","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-03T08:32:44Z","updated_at":"2024-12-03T08:32:44Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"@melonora let me know if you have time to pair in Dec or Jan to get this over the line. References and relevant issues
From discussion on image.sc forum:
https://forum.image.sc/t/napari-labels-layers-borders-displays-with-weird-shading/105499/1

# Description
This modifies the 3D isosurface shader for the Labels layer in a few ways. The goal is to improve appearance of the 3D rendered labels. Specifically it should be improved at the boundaries of the volume (for both `fast` and `smooth` gradient modes) and for 1px thin labels (for `smooth` gradient mode).

Changes include:
* A new uniform to toggle what happens when sampling _outside_ the volume:
  * clamping to the edge (default, current behavior)
  * returning the background value
* Update code to prevent dark surfaces in very thin (1px) labels
  * Add check for adjacent background pixels to the `smooth` gradient function
  * Update the gradient calculation to include an estimate of the local gradient instead of setting a default lambert term

The configurable clamping behavior has the effect of giving labels "dark" (almost hollow-looking) or "filled" faces at the borders of the label volume. Currently this is controlled by a property on the `Volume` visual, but toggling is not exposed in the UI. I think at least @jni is requesting to be able to toggle this in the UI. I'm happy to wire that up in this PR if there's agreement, but I could use input on a name.

![labels-edges-pr](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/728505be-d212-417b-a29e-7228761ffed3)

I did a quick benchmark test on my machine and did not see significant performance hit. I will try to do a longer test as well.

Finally, I need to look a bit into what the "background value" is. Right now it's hard-coded in the shader, but it sounds like perhaps the `Labels` layer may be able to have a non-zero background. I want to make sure to support that or at least not cause a regression.

# Description

Change `mode` following `visible` attribute value (changing `mode` to be pan/zoom if the layer is not visible and saving the previous mode to restore it when the layer is visible again) and prevent changing `mode` to something different to pan/zoom while the layer is not visible:

![label_mode_visible](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/13736aa5-c06d-45c6-9823-623a61417c3c)

🐛 Bug Report This bug has been found to happen across machines running Windows 11 and there are no such issues when run on a workstation running on RedHat 8/9 When a new shape is created in a shape layer and then one toggles off the visibility of the shapes layer, any changes in the viewer results in an error to be thrown. This becomes a hinderance when there is an image or other layers open as the error causes the image to flicker. One thing I noticed is that the issue is solved (the flickering and error stops) when I click on the Pan/zoom button in the disabled shapes layer. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fee33b07-31d9-49e4-b926-733299b25c02 ### 💡 Steps to Reproduce 1. Create shape layer 2. Add a rectangle 3. Hide the shape layer 4. Change the view by clicking on the mouse and then dragging ### 💡 Expected Behavior The expectation was to have the viewer behave normally and the image would move as per the mouse drag ### 🌎 {"repository_id":144513571,"push_id":21533331608,"size":1,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/main","head":"14d70238e3081cf348263a66de3a5f275ff8c998","before":"57217049649b3e1a612b55717dc3df129367bfed","commits":[{"sha":"14d70238e3081cf348263a66de3a5f275ff8c998","author":{"email":"b9319e2414749837dadc4d179d8c41f306ae7b76@users.noreply.github.com","name":"Daniel Althviz Moré"},"message":"Prevent errors when interacting with selected non-visible layers (#7418)

# References and relevant issues

Closes https://github.com/napari/napari/issues/7314
Closes https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/7348 (supersedes)

# Description

Change `mode` following `visible` attribute value (changing `mode` to be
pan/zoom if the layer is not visible and saving the previous mode to
restore it when the layer is visible again) and prevent changing `mode`
to something different to pan/zoom while the layer is not visible:


![label_mode_visible](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/13736aa5-c06d-45c6-9823-623a61417c3c)","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/commits/14d70238e3081cf348263a66de3a5f275ff8c998"}]}

{"action":"closed","number":7348,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/pulls/7348","id":2158873271,"node_id":"PR_kwDOCJ0aI86Arcq3","html_url":"https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/7348","diff_url":"https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/7348.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/7348.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/7348","number":7348,"state":"closed","locked":false,"title":"Explore ways to prevent errors when interacting with selected non-visible layers","user":{"login":"dalthviz","id":16781833,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE2NzgxODMz","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/16781833?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/dalthviz","html_url":"https://github.com/dalthviz","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dalthviz/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dalthviz/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dalthviz/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dalthviz/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dalthviz/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dalthviz/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dalthviz/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dalthviz/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dalthviz/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"# References and relevant issues

Related with #7314

# Description

## Approach 1
Set the layer's `editable` attribute following the layer's `visible` attribute to prevent being able to interact/edit the layer while is hidden.

A preview (when a layer is hidden the layer mode gets now set to the pan/zoom mode since is not editable):

<details>

![toggling_visibility_editability](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/565bb0a8-9a0b-4c2b-b2ff-6f38a591a69a)

</details>

https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/7348/commits/6585c3246dfcc500bbf4e09ddd62a744aabf01ff

Note: Partially discarted since there are already in place some tests that check for the independence of attribute values (setting `visible` should not change the `editable` value) and also does not restore mode being used when the layer was visible (always makes the mode end up being pan/zoom).

## Approach 2

Change `mode` following `visible` attribute value (changing `mode` to be pan/zoom if the layer is not visible and saving the previous mode to restore it when the layer is visible again) and prevent changing `mode` to something different to pan/zoom while the layer is not visible:

<details>

![label_mode_visible](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/13736aa5-c06d-45c6-9823-623a61417c3c)

</details>

https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/7348/commits/d127bcd4c340ccc8968676d731c9c6855692df1d

## Approach 3

Don't mess with attributes values dependencies (`visible` x `mode` x `editable`) and just disable mouse bindings if a layer is not visible (and add validations around in case values being used for layer computations end up being `None`)

A preview:

<details>

![non-visible-layer-interactions](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/433290b6-9f3d-42b6-b851-fc54a4dd2215)

</details>

https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/7348/commits/5642a88799426332d173045d0085350ecd8c5532

# General notes

* Regardless of the approach to implement the fix, the desire functionality seems to be not being able to interact/change a layer that is not visible (even when is still selected) while also preserving the state in regard of mode selected once the layer is visible again. An example user flow (explaining current behavior and desired one) is described at https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/7348#issuecomment-2456053311

{"action":"closed","number":7348,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/pulls/7348","id":2158873271,"node_id":"PR_kwDOCJ0aI86Arcq3","html_url":"https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/7348","diff_url":"https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/7348.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/7348.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/7348","number":7348,"state":"closed","locked":false,"title":"Explore ways to prevent errors when interacting with selected non-visible layers","user":{"login":"dalthviz","id":16781833,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE2NzgxODMz","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/16781833?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/dalthviz","html_url":"https://github.com/dalthviz","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dalthviz/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dalthviz/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dalthviz/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dalthviz/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dalthviz/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dalthviz/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dalthviz/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dalthviz/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dalthviz/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"# References and relevant issues

Related with #7314

# Description

## Approach 1
Set the layer's `editable` attribute following the layer's `visible` attribute to prevent being able to interact/edit the layer while is hidden.

A preview (when a layer is hidden the layer mode gets now set to the pan/zoom mode since is not editable):

<details>

![toggling_visibility_editability](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/565bb0a8-9a0b-4c2b-b2ff-6f38a591a69a)

</details>

https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/7348/commits/6585c3246dfcc500bbf4e09ddd62a744aabf01ff

Note: Partially discarted since there are already in place some tests that check for the independence of attribute values (setting `visible` should not change the `editable` value) and also does not restore mode being used when the layer was visible (always makes the mode end up being pan/zoom).

## Approach 2

Change `mode` following `visible` attribute value (changing `mode` to be pan/zoom if the layer is not visible and saving the previous mode to restore it when the layer is visible again) and prevent changing `mode` to something different to pan/zoom while the layer is not visible:

<details>

![label_mode_visible](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/13736aa5-c06d-45c6-9823-623a61417c3c)

</details>

https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/7348/commits/d127bcd4c340ccc8968676d731c9c6855692df1d

## Approach 3

Don't mess with attributes values dependencies (`visible` x `mode` x `editable`) and just disable mouse bindings if a layer is not visible (and add validations around in case values being used for layer computations end up being `None`)

A preview:

<details>

![non-visible-layer-interactions](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/433290b6-9f3d-42b6-b851-fc54a4dd2215)

</details>

https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/7348/commits/5642a88799426332d173045d0085350ecd8c5532

# General notes

* Regardless of the approach to implement the fix, the desire functionality seems to be not being able to interact/change a layer that is not visible (even when is still selected) while also preserving the state in regard of mode selected once the layer is visible again. An example user flow (explaining current behavior and desired one) is described at https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/7348#issuecomment-2456053311

Addresses / Closes? #7425 

# Description

Examples using `screenshot` and `figure_export` were found to have a grayscale canvas in the docs. The grayscale appears to be from the `flash=True` argument, so this PR sets `flash=False` to fix the display of the viewer for the Gallery. The canvas will now properly be color. 

This does not fix the root cause of the grayscale canvas as a result of `flash=True`.

Tested with a local docs build (finally!)

Thank you @psobolewskiPhD for assistance.

{"repository_id":144513571,"push_id":21533881707,"size":1,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/main","head":"d95aab7f14841bc28a1304c03e716386e707a0fd","before":"14d70238e3081cf348263a66de3a5f275ff8c998","commits":[{"sha":"d95aab7f14841bc28a1304c03e716386e707a0fd","author":{"email":"c138c54681810bb6877b17d754e13e3ddbf09122@gmail.com","name":"Tim Monko"},"message":"Turn off 'flash' in `screenshot`/`export_figure` examples (#7427)

# References and relevant issues

Addresses / Closes? #7425 

# Description

Examples using `screenshot` and `figure_export` were found to have a
grayscale canvas in the docs. The grayscale appears to be from the
`flash=True` argument, so this PR sets `flash=False` to fix the display
of the viewer for the Gallery. The canvas will now properly be color.

This does not fix the root cause of the grayscale canvas as a result of
`flash=True`.

Tested with a local docs build (finally!)

Thank you @psobolewskiPhD for assistance.","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/commits/d95aab7f14841bc28a1304c03e716386e707a0fd"}]}

 Export Figure
 =============
 
-Display one shapes layer ontop of one image layer using the ``add_shapes`` and
-``add_image`` APIs. When the window is closed it will print the coordinates of
-your shapes.
+Display a variety of layer types in the napari viewer and export the figure with `viewer.export_figure()`.
+The exported figure is then added back as an image layer.
+
+Exported figures include the extent of all data in 2D view, and does not presently work for 3D views.
+To capture the extent of the canvas, instead of the layers, see `viewer.screenshot()`: `examples/to_screenshot.py` and `examples/screenshot_and_export_figure.py`.","path":"examples/export_figure.py","commit_id":"b9e56d31d8d7ae962d1c08ae53255eec0641cffc","original_commit_id":"b9e56d31d8d7ae962d1c08ae53255eec0641cffc","user":{"login":"jni","id":492549,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ5MjU0OQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/492549?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni","html_url":"https://github.com/jni","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"@melissawm how would such links work? rendering","user":{"login":"aganders3","id":1231828,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEyMzE4Mjg=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1231828?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3","html_url":"https://github.com/aganders3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":5,"created_at":"2024-12-03T21:52:16Z","updated_at":"2024-12-05T22:31:13Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/pulls/7431","html_url":"https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/7431","diff_url":"https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/7431.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/7431.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"# References and relevant issues
From discussion on image.sc forum:
https://forum.image.sc/t/napari-labels-layers-borders-displays-with-weird-shading/105499/1

# Description
This modifies the 3D isosurface shader for the Labels layer in a few ways. The goal is to improve appearance of the 3D rendered labels. Specifically it should be improved at the boundaries of the volume (for both `fast` and `smooth` gradient modes) and for 1px thin labels (for `smooth` gradient mode).

Changes include:
* A new uniform to toggle what happens when sampling _outside_ the volume:
  * clamping to the edge (default, current behavior)
  * returning the background value
* Update code to prevent dark surfaces in very thin (1px) labels
  * Add check for adjacent background pixels to the `smooth` gradient function
  * Update the gradient calculation to include an estimate of the local gradient instead of setting a default lambert term

The configurable clamping behavior has the effect of giving labels "dark" (almost hollow-looking) or "filled" faces at the borders of the label volume. Currently this is controlled by a property on the `Volume` visual, but toggling is not exposed in the UI. I think at least @jni is requesting to be able to toggle this in the UI. In the animation below this is what the floating magicgui widget is toggling. I'm happy to wire that up in this PR if there's agreement, but I could use input on a name. We also need to choose a default.

![labels-edges-pr](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/728505be-d212-417b-a29e-7228761ffed3)

I did a quick benchmark test on my machine and did not see significant performance hit. I will try to do a longer test as well.

Finally, I need to look a bit into what the "background value" is. Right now it's hard-coded in the shader, but it sounds like perhaps the `Labels` layer may be able to have a non-zero background. I want to make sure to support that or at least not cause a regression.

I did a quick benchmark test on my machine and did not see significant performance hit. I will try to do a longer test as well.

Finally, I need to look a bit into what the "background value" is. Right now it's hard-coded in the shader, but it sounds like perhaps the `Labels` layer may be able to have a non-zero background. I want to make sure to support that or at least not cause a regression.

rendering","user":{"login":"aganders3","id":1231828,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEyMzE4Mjg=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1231828?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3","html_url":"https://github.com/aganders3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"# References and relevant issues
From discussion on image.sc forum:
https://forum.image.sc/t/napari-labels-layers-borders-displays-with-weird-shading/105499/1

# Description
This modifies the 3D isosurface shader for the Labels layer in a few ways. The goal is to improve appearance of the 3D rendered labels. Specifically it should be improved at the boundaries of the volume (for both `fast` and `smooth` gradient modes) and for 1px thin labels (for `smooth` gradient mode).

Changes include:
* A new uniform to toggle what happens when sampling _outside_ the volume:
  * clamping to the edge (default, current behavior)
  * returning the background value
* Update code to prevent dark surfaces in very thin (1px) labels
  * Add check for adjacent background pixels to the `smooth` gradient function
  * Update the gradient calculation to include an estimate of the local gradient instead of setting a default lambert term

The configurable clamping behavior has the effect of giving labels "dark" (almost hollow-looking) or "filled" faces at the borders of the label volume. Currently this is controlled by a property on the `Volume` visual, but toggling is not exposed in the UI. I think at least @jni is requesting to be able to toggle this in the UI. In the animation below this is what the floating magicgui widget is toggling. I'm happy to wire that up in this PR if there's agreement, but I could use input on a name. We also need to choose a default.

![labels-edges-pr](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/728505be-d212-417b-a29e-7228761ffed3)

I did a quick benchmark test on my machine and did not see significant performance hit. I will try to do a longer test as well.

Finally, I need to look a bit into what the "background value" is. Right now it's hard-coded in the shader, but it sounds like perhaps the `Labels` layer may be able to have a non-zero background. I want to make sure to support that or at least not cause a regression.

rendering","user":{"login":"aganders3","id":1231828,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEyMzE4Mjg=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1231828?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3","html_url":"https://github.com/aganders3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"# References and relevant issues
From discussion on image.sc forum:
https://forum.image.sc/t/napari-labels-layers-borders-displays-with-weird-shading/105499/1

# Description
This modifies the 3D isosurface shader for the Labels layer in a few ways. The goal is to improve appearance of the 3D rendered labels. Specifically it should be improved at the boundaries of the volume (for both `fast` and `smooth` gradient modes) and for 1px thin labels (for `smooth` gradient mode).

Changes include:
* A new uniform to toggle what happens when sampling _outside_ the volume:
  * clamping to the edge (default, current behavior)
  * returning the background value
* Update code to prevent dark surfaces in very thin (1px) labels
  * Add check for adjacent background pixels to the `smooth` gradient function
  * Update the gradient calculation to include an estimate of the local gradient instead of setting a default lambert term

The configurable clamping behavior has the effect of giving labels "dark" (almost hollow-looking) or "filled" faces at the borders of the label volume. Currently this is controlled by a property on the `Volume` visual, but toggling is not exposed in the UI. I think at least @jni is requesting to be able to toggle this in the UI. In the animation below this is what the floating magicgui widget is toggling. I'm happy to wire that up in this PR if there's agreement, but I could use input on a name. We also need to choose a default.

![labels-edges-pr](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/728505be-d212-417b-a29e-7228761ffed3)

I did a quick benchmark test on my machine and did not see significant performance hit. I will try to do a longer test as well.

Finally, I need to look a bit into what the "background value" is. Right now it's hard-coded in the shader, but it sounds like perhaps the `Labels` layer may be able to have a non-zero background. I want to make sure to support that or at least not cause a regression.

+# Troubleshooting
+
+This page collects a few known issues and solutions for common problems that users might encounter when using napari.
+If you are facing an error that is not covered by this page, please
+[open an issue](https://github.com/napari/napari/issues/new) in the napari repository.
+
+## Known issues
+
+### Resetting preferences and settings
+
+Changing napari versions—or conda envs with different napari versions—can cause issues with settings. These issues can
+be solved by resetting the settings back to default using the terminal command:
+
+```python
+napari --reset
+```
+
+### torch + rosetta + napari crashes Python
+
+In some cases, when using napari on Mac M1 using Rosetta you may experience crashing when adding layers or using a script.
+If you observe this, you can try to run napari using the native arm64 Python interpreter (see [napari#7259](https://github.com/napari/napari/issues/7259).
+
+### "module napari has no attribute Viewer"
+
+This may happen when doing an editable install on top of a normal one, or when you have a local file called `napari.py`.
+
+To fix this, either rename the file or remove all previous napari versions in your environment before installing the editable version.
+
+### My image renders as all black/all white
+
+This can happen when the contrast limits are not set correctly. You can reset the contrast limits by right-clicking "contras limits", then clicking the "Reset" button in the dialog shown.
+
+### napari + numba combination doesn't work on admin installs of Windows
+
+If you have trouble using napari together with numba on Windows, try setting `NUMBA_CACHE_DIR` to an user-accessible location.
+
+See [napari#7288](https://github.com/napari/napari/issues/7288).
+
+## FAQ
+
+### How do I use napari in a remote Jupyter env?
+
+napari is a native Qt app so it's not possible to do this directly within a Jupyter notebook or JupyterLab tab. However, we are working on documenting options for this 🧰 Task

The View menu has the ability to toggle scale and axes, because there are viewer things existing in the canvas space.
We've not had a good way to provide similar access to Layer things other than the layer list contextual menu, which is quite busy already. Is the new Layers menu a good way to explore that?

🐛 Bug Report

When using Ctrl+S (on Linux) to try to save a layer with no layer selected through the GUI, an OSError appears which is not very friendly to users.

### 💡 Steps to Reproduce

1. Create any layer on the gui
2. Click Ctrl+S (Linux/Windows) or Command+S (Mac)
3. Observe the OSError

### 💡 Expected Behavior

Maybe a dialog with a friendlier "No layer selected. Please select a layer to save" message.

### 🌎 Environment

napari dev
Manjaro Linux + KDE
Python 3.11

### 💡 Additional Context

If you have 1 RGB layer say, the distinction between selected and not gets kinda blurred--you can interact with it, pan zoom, etc. It's safe to assume in this case Command-S should save that layer or remind you to click it.

This potentially could be done by making a new context key, something like "last_active_layer" or "last_active_layer_selection", which would basically ignore any empty selections? But I think it's critical that there is a single code path, and the keybinding and menu both follow that one code path.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/comments/2522006891/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

+# Troubleshooting
+
+This page collects a few known issues and solutions for common problems that users might encounter when using napari.
+If you are facing an error that is not covered by this page, please
+[open an issue](https://github.com/napari/napari/issues/new) in the napari repository.
+
+## Known issues
+
+### Resetting preferences and settings
+
+Changing napari versions—or conda envs with different napari versions—can cause issues with settings. These issues can
+be solved by resetting the settings back to default using the terminal command:
+
+```python
+napari --reset
+```
+
+### torch + rosetta + napari crashes Python
+
+In some cases, when using napari on Mac M1 using Rosetta you may experience crashing when adding layers or using a script.
+If you observe this, you can try to run napari using the native arm64 Python interpreter (see [napari#7259](https://github.com/napari/napari/issues/7259).","path":"docs/troubleshooting.md","commit_id":"e80cd2c279ea4cc0cb785b946c1f61ffe501bd02","original_commit_id":"e80cd2c279ea4cc0cb785b946c1f61ffe501bd02","user":{"login":"jni","id":492549,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ5MjU0OQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/492549?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni","html_url":"https://github.com/jni","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"```suggestion

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/docs/pulls/comments/1872503521","pull_request_review_id":2483510196,"id":1872503521,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOINmmMM5vnCLh","diff_hunk":"@@ -0,0 +1,43 @@
+# Troubleshooting
+
+This page collects a few known issues and solutions for common problems that users might encounter when using napari.
+If you are facing an error that is not covered by this page, please
+[open an issue](https://github.com/napari/napari/issues/new) in the napari repository.
+
+## Known issues
+
+### Resetting preferences and settings
+
+Changing napari versions—or conda envs with different napari versions—can cause issues with settings. These issues can
+be solved by resetting the settings back to default using the terminal command:
+
+```python
+napari --reset
+```
+
+### torch + rosetta + napari crashes Python
+
+In some cases, when using napari on Mac M1 using Rosetta you may experience crashing when adding layers or using a script.
+If you observe this, you can try to run napari using the native arm64 Python interpreter (see [napari#7259](https://github.com/napari/napari/issues/7259).
+
+### "module napari has no attribute Viewer"
+
+This may happen when doing an editable install on top of a normal one, or when you have a local file called `napari.py`.
+
+To fix this, either rename the file or remove all previous napari versions in your environment before installing the editable version.
+
+### My image renders as all black/all white
+
+This can happen when the contrast limits are not set correctly. You can reset the contrast limits by right-clicking "contras limits", then clicking the "Reset" button in the dialog shown.","path":"docs/troubleshooting.md","commit_id":"e80cd2c279ea4cc0cb785b946c1f61ffe501bd02","original_commit_id":"e80cd2c279ea4cc0cb785b946c1f61ffe501bd02","user":{"login":"jni","id":492549,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ5MjU0OQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/492549?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni","html_url":"https://github.com/jni","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"```suggestion
This can happen when the contrast limits are not set correctly. You can reset the contrast limits by right-clicking "contrast limits", then clicking the "Reset" button in the dialog shown.
```

But I think the advice should be more extensive here:

> The contrast limits are the minimum and maximum values displayed by the layer. Any value under the smaller contrast limit will appear as black, while any value above the larger limit will appear as white. Additionally, when all the values are *near* the bottom of the range, the image can still appear black even if there are some very dark but not quite black pixels there.
> 
> You can reset the contrast limits by clicking the Auto-Contrast: "Once" button, or, if that fails, right clicking on the contrast limits to do manual adjustment, including directly editing the numbers shown. When in doubt, you can hover over the canvas with your image layer selected, and check the status message as you move the mouse around. That will give you an idea of the range and variability of the data in your layer, helping you set meaningful contrast limits.

This maybe points to writing a more extensive contrast limits and colormaps tutorial that we can link to from here, but otherwise I think it's worth doing a couple of paragraphs to help people understand what they are reading. (Screenshots would also be good here I +# Troubleshooting
+
+This page collects a few known issues and solutions for common problems that users might encounter when using napari.
+If you are facing an error that is not covered by this page, please
+[open an issue](https://github.com/napari/napari/issues/new) in the napari repository.
+
+## Known issues
+
+### Resetting preferences and settings
+
+Changing napari versions—or conda envs with different napari versions—can cause issues with settings. These issues can
+be solved by resetting the settings back to default using the terminal command:
+
+```python
+napari --reset
+```
+
+### torch + rosetta + napari crashes Python
+
+In some cases, when using napari on Mac M1 using Rosetta you may experience crashing when adding layers or using a script.
+If you observe this, you can try to run napari using the native arm64 Python interpreter (see [napari#7259](https://github.com/napari/napari/issues/7259).
+
+### "module napari has no attribute Viewer"
+
+This may happen when doing an editable install on top of a normal one, or when you have a local file called `napari.py`.
+
+To fix this, either rename the file or remove all previous napari versions in your environment before installing the editable version.
+
+### My image renders as all black/all white
+
+This can happen when the contrast limits are not set correctly. You can reset the contrast limits by right-clicking "contras limits", then clicking the "Reset" button in the dialog shown.
+
+### napari + numba combination doesn't work on admin installs of Windows
+
+If you have trouble using napari together with numba on Windows, try setting `NUMBA_CACHE_DIR` to an user-accessible location.
+
+See Z","updated_at":"2024-12-06T03:05:20Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/pulls/7355","html_url":"https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/7355","diff_url":"https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/7355.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/7355.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"# References and relevant issues

Related with PR #6219

# Description

This takes out some of the refactoring effort proposed over PR #6219 (creation of classes that encapsulate specific widget controls for logic reusage and provide base methods to add those widgets to layer controls classes).

# Notes

* ~For the moment,~ this ~only~ covers the shape layer, labels layer, image layer, surface layer and tracks layer controls
* There are a couple of widgets definitions that maybe could be further refactored like for example the ones related with the layer `colormap` attribute (widgets inside `qt_colormap_control`).
* While checking this I noticed a couple of issues:
  * https://github.com/napari/napari/issues/7393
  * https://github.com/napari/napari/issues/7394
* Also, noticed that over the Vectors layer controls a `raise` is being call when handling a `TypeError` exception (when a edge color mode doesn't correspond with the current valid values). This causes the error to be shown to the user eventhough a fallback to put set a proper value is done. Could it make sense to make an issue about that? 🤔

# Description

submatrix from the layer transform matrix and using that. The OP of

🐛 Bug Report

If you have a 3D layer with scale not (1, 1, 1), then when using the `o`/`oblique` button, the plane normal isn't correctly set.
I think this is because camera.view_direction is in world coordinates:
https://github.com/napari/napari/blob/0e01840c9237489acf238aec1d5cdaf9201ed640/napari/components/camera.py#L58-L63
and `plane.normal` is in data coordinates, but the transformation used for the oblique feature uses `_world_to_displayed_data_ray` rather than transforming the normal:
https://github.com/napari/napari/blob/0e01840c9237489acf238aec1d5cdaf9201ed640/napari/layers/image/_image_key_bindings.py#L45-L58

See the implementation here in napari-threedee:
https://github.com/napari-threedee/napari-threedee/blob/6674afe70938724cbaffc0677d2dfd804993bdba/src/napari_threedee/utils/napari_utils.py#L222
And a helpful explanation:
https://www.scratchapixel.com/lessons/mathematics-physics-for-computer-graphics/geometry/transforming-normals.html

### 💡 Steps to Reproduce

```
import napari
from napari.layers.image._image_key_bindings import orient_plane_normal_along_view_direction_no_gen
from skimage import data
import numpy as np

cells = data.cells3d()

viewer, layer = napari.imshow(cells[:,1,...])
layer.scale = (4, 1, 1)
viewer.dims.ndisplay = 3
layer.depiction = 'plane'
layer.plane.position = (25, 170, 50)
layer.bounding_box.visible = True

viewer.camera.set_view_direction((0.98, 0, .21))
orient_plane_normal_along_view_direction_no_gen(layer)

viewer.update_console(locals())

napari.run()
```

![image](https://github.com/napari/napari/assets/76622105/55ebadb1-0973-49f4-90c2-7d06b9b2ab25)


### 💡 Expected Behavior

The proper plane orientation:
```
import napari
from napari.layers.image._image_key_bindings import orient_plane_normal_along_view_direction_no_gen
from skimage import data
import numpy as np

cells = data.cells3d()

viewer, layer = napari.imshow(cells[:,1,...])
layer.scale = (4, 1, 1)
viewer.dims.ndisplay = 3
layer.depiction = 'plane'
layer.plane.position = (25, 170, 50)
layer.bounding_box.visible = True

viewer.camera.set_view_direction((0.98, 0, .21))

# from napari-threedee
def world_to_data_normal(vector, layer):
    """Convert a normal vector defining an orientation from world coordinates to data coordinates.
    For example, this would be used to a plane normal.

    https://www.scratchapixel.com/lessons/mathematics-physics-for-computer-graphics/geometry/transforming-normals.html

    Parameters
    ----------
    vector : tuple, list, 1D array
        A vector in world coordinates.
    layer : napari.layers.BaseLayer
        The napari layer to get the transform from.

    Returns
    -------
    np.ndarray
        Transformed vector in data coordinates. This returns a unit vector.
    """
    unit_vector = np.asarray(vector) / np.linalg.norm(vector)

    # get the transform
    # the napari transform is from layer -> world.
    # We want the inverse of the world ->  layer, so we just take the napari transform
    inverse_transform = layer._transforms[1:].simplified.linear_matrix
    transpose_inverse_transform = inverse_transform.T

    # transform the vector
    transformed_vector = np.matmul(transpose_inverse_transform, unit_vector)

    return transformed_vector / np.linalg.norm(transformed_vector)

layer.plane.normal = world_to_data_normal(viewer.camera.view_direction, layer)

napari.run()
```

![image](https://github.com/napari/napari/assets/76622105/94f3e775-38a9-463d-b682-1f62e253f0eb)

rendering","user":{"login":"aganders3","id":1231828,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEyMzE4Mjg=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1231828?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3","html_url":"https://github.com/aganders3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"# References and relevant issues
From discussion on image.sc forum:
https://forum.image.sc/t/napari-labels-layers-borders-displays-with-weird-shading/105499/1

# Description
This modifies the 3D isosurface shader for the Labels layer in a few ways. The goal is to improve appearance of the 3D rendered labels. Specifically it should be improved at the boundaries of the volume (for both `fast` and `smooth` gradient modes) and for 1px thin labels (for `smooth` gradient mode).

Changes include:
* A new uniform to toggle what happens when sampling _outside_ the volume:
  * clamping to the edge (default, current behavior)
  * returning the background value
* Update code to prevent dark surfaces in very thin (1px) labels
  * Add check for adjacent background pixels to the `smooth` gradient function
  * Update the gradient calculation to include an estimate of the local gradient instead of setting a default lambert term

The configurable clamping behavior has the effect of giving labels "dark" (almost hollow-looking) or "filled" faces at the borders of the label volume. Currently this is controlled by a property on the `Volume` visual, but toggling is not exposed in the UI. I think at least @jni is requesting to be able to toggle this in the UI. In the animation below this is what the floating magicgui widget is toggling. I'm happy to wire that up in this PR if there's agreement, but I could use input on a name. We also need to choose a default.

![labels-edges-pr](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/728505be-d212-417b-a29e-7228761ffed3)

I did a quick benchmark test on my machine and did not see significant performance hit. I will try to do a longer test as well.

Finally, I need to look a bit into what the "background value" is. Right now it's hard-coded in the shader, but it sounds like perhaps the `Labels` layer may be able to have a non-zero background. I want to make sure to support that or at least not cause a regression.

rendering","user":{"login":"aganders3","id":1231828,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEyMzE4Mjg=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1231828?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3","html_url":"https://github.com/aganders3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"# References and relevant issues
From discussion on image.sc forum:
https://forum.image.sc/t/napari-labels-layers-borders-displays-with-weird-shading/105499/1

# Description
This modifies the 3D isosurface shader for the Labels layer in a few ways. The goal is to improve appearance of the 3D rendered labels. Specifically it should be improved at the boundaries of the volume (for both `fast` and `smooth` gradient modes) and for 1px thin labels (for `smooth` gradient mode).

Changes include:
* A new uniform to toggle what happens when sampling _outside_ the volume:
  * clamping to the edge (default, current behavior)
  * returning the background value
* Update code to prevent dark surfaces in very thin (1px) labels
  * Add check for adjacent background pixels to the `smooth` gradient function
  * Update the gradient calculation to include an estimate of the local gradient instead of setting a default lambert term

The configurable clamping behavior has the effect of giving labels "dark" (almost hollow-looking) or "filled" faces at the borders of the label volume. Currently this is controlled by a property on the `Volume` visual, but toggling is not exposed in the UI. I think at least @jni is requesting to be able to toggle this in the UI. In the animation below this is what the floating magicgui widget is toggling. I'm happy to wire that up in this PR if there's agreement, but I could use input on a name. We also need to choose a default.

![labels-edges-pr](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/728505be-d212-417b-a29e-7228761ffed3)

I did a quick benchmark test on my machine and did not see significant performance hit. I will try to do a longer test as well.

Finally, I need to look a bit into what the "background value" is. Right now it's hard-coded in the shader, but it sounds like perhaps the `Labels` layer may be able to have a non-zero background. I want to make sure to support that or at least not cause a regression.

rendering","user":{"login":"aganders3","id":1231828,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEyMzE4Mjg=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1231828?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3","html_url":"https://github.com/aganders3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aganders3/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"# References and relevant issues
From discussion on image.sc forum:
https://forum.image.sc/t/napari-labels-layers-borders-displays-with-weird-shading/105499/1

# Description
This modifies the 3D isosurface shader for the Labels layer in a few ways. The goal is to improve appearance of the 3D rendered labels. Specifically it should be improved at the boundaries of the volume (for both `fast` and `smooth` gradient modes) and for 1px thin labels (for `smooth` gradient mode).

Changes include:
* A new uniform to toggle what happens when sampling _outside_ the volume:
  * clamping to the edge (default, current behavior)
  * returning the background value
* Update code to prevent dark surfaces in very thin (1px) labels
  * Add check for adjacent background pixels to the `smooth` gradient function
  * Update the gradient calculation to include an estimate of the local gradient instead of setting a default lambert term

The configurable clamping behavior has the effect of giving labels "dark" (almost hollow-looking) or "filled" faces at the borders of the label volume. Currently this is controlled by a property on the `Volume` visual, but toggling is not exposed in the UI. I think at least @jni is requesting to be able to toggle this in the UI. In the animation below this is what the floating magicgui widget is toggling. I'm happy to wire that up in this PR if there's agreement, but I could use input on a name. We also need to choose a default.

![labels-edges-pr](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/728505be-d212-417b-a29e-7228761ffed3)

I did a quick benchmark test on my machine and did not see significant performance hit. I will try to do a longer test as well.

Finally, I need to look a bit into what the "background value" is. Right now it's hard-coded in the shader, but it sounds like perhaps the `Labels` layer may be able to have a non-zero background. I want to make sure to support that or at least not cause a regression.

References and relevant issues # Description Similar to #7256 this PR is a speedup of face triangulation by avoiding a lot of reallocation of arrays. After long investigation, I found that using best way to speed this up is by avoiding the allocation of additional arrays by using numba. For shapes that do not have bevels: On main: ![obraz](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b6775d94-a88b-4a99-959f-f4b6f7f768aa) In this PR: ![obraz](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9a3a302c-2eb1-4ecd-8c38-a07617512ede) PR with cache: ![obraz](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6d7f00a8-4d55-4bb2-9f49-955bc746e01f) As you may see, the time of `triangulate_edge` is reduced from 44s to 17s. Half of it is numba compilation. With sharp edges that require bevel join, the speedup is even bigger: Main: ![obraz](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e2a55f13-6ba7-4f1d-9ff9-56fa46fdf49c) PR: ![obraz](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6279350e-1e50-441e-a538-4f01b5420d40) PR with cache: ![obraz](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3c992928-b63c-42d7-be6d-d0ec7ec620f5) This PR also fixes the bevel join On main: ![obraz](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9a24bc31-9777-4d60-81d5-ff6cc1b0d1a2) With PR: ![obraz](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b167a4d5-9bce-442c-80c6-d5a0c90e5f8d) In this PR I have added `examples/dev/triangle_edge.py` with a set of helper functions to visualize triangulation. These are helpful for debugging of triangulation problems. # Algorithm explanation Here I add some explanation to the code implementation of this PR. ## Calculation of miter vector # This is a screenshot of miter join: ![obraz](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/08d2dd78-bb02-4da8-baa5-7b0596e1c3c7) And let's assume markings as on image: ![obraz](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d0cec9ff-3786-4edc-805a-d45e58f33aab) The $\vec{BA}$ (blue) is half of the normal vector from vertex $n$ to $n+1$. The $\vec{AC}$ is minus half of the normal vector from vertex $n-1$ to $n$. The vector $\vec{AD}$ (green) is orthogonal to line $BA$ of length $0.5$ Based on these vectors, we could easily calculate $\vec{BC} = \vec{BA} + \vec{AC}$. However, the length of $|GC|$ may be different from 1. We would like to get the vector $\vec{BE}$ We will use here trigonometry and [Intercept theorem](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Intercept_theorem) We know that $\frac{|GC|}{|AC|} = \sin{\alpha}$ and $|AC| = \frac{1}{2}$ so $|GC| = \frac{\sin{\alpha}}{2}$ From intercept theorem, we know that $\frac{|BC|}{|GC|} = \frac{|BE|}{|FE|}$. The length of $|FE| = \frac{1}{2}$. So $|BE| = |BC| \times \frac{1}{\sin{\alpha}}$. That mens that $\vec{BE} = \vec{BC} \times \frac{1}{\sin{\alpha}}$ ## Bevel limit # In general, we use miter join, however, if the angle is sharp, it may result in strange artifacts (see #6706). So if the join will be too long (The length of $\vec{BA}$ vector from above), we would like to change to a bevel join. So we need to calculate the length of $\vec{BE}$. We would like to avoid calculation of square root in every turn of the loop. From Pythagoras theorem we know that $|BE|^2 = |EF|^2 + |BF|^2 = \frac{1}{2}^2 + |BF|^2$ We also know that $|GA| = \frac{\cos{\alpha}}{2}$ so $|BG| = \frac{1 - \cos{\alpha}}{2}$. From miter vector calculation and intercept theorem we know that $|BF| = \frac{|BG|}{\sin{\alpha}} = \frac{1 - \cos{\alpha}}{2\sin{\alpha}}$ So we know the length of the vector $$|BE|^2 = \frac{1}{2}^2 + \left(\frac{1 - \cos{\alpha}}{2\sin{\alpha}}\right)^2 = \frac{\sin^2{\alpha} + 1^2 - 2\cos{\alpha} + \cos^2{\alpha}}{4\sin^2{\alpha}} = \frac{2 - 2 \cos{\alpha}}{4\sin^2{\alpha}} $$ Using trigonometric identity $$|BE|^2 = \frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{1 - \cos{\alpha}}{1 - \cos^2{\alpha}} = \frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{1 - \cos{\alpha}}{(1 - \cos{\alpha})(1 + \cos{\alpha})} = \frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{1+\cos{\alpha}}$$ So if we would like to check condition $|BE| > limit$ we could use the following equation: $$ \frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{1+\cos{\alpha}} > {limit}^2$$ So: $$ \frac{1}{2 \cdot {limit}^2} - 1 > \cos{\alpha} $$ It means that if $\cos\alpha$ is below a given threshold, we should use bevel join. ## Too long vector in bevel join # As I showed above, this PR is fixing the bevel join by fixing the position of inner edge of the bevel join. You may see an example in this image (see the longest yellow vector): ![obraz](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/58e13821-a1b6-4dd0-a49b-ade97edf9e73) The schematic of generating long edge: ![obraz](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/329b2b61-3c9d-4050-9623-7053c1f46248) As users may use shapes with different length edges, we cannot use a hard-coded limit for the length of the offset vector that creates the problems pointed on above image. However, when calculating the normal vectors, we need to calculate the edge width, so we may save them for later usage and limit the length of such problematic offset vector to length of shortest adjusted edge. In the Bevel limit section, we have an explicit formula for miter offset length. However, it contains a square root. And calculation of a square root is expensive on current CPU, so we would like to avoid it. However, the described problem happens only when $\measuredangle BAC$ is close to $180\degree$, but in such a situation, the $|\vec{BA} + \vec{AC}|$ is close to 1 (as resulted vector is close to diameter). So we could approximate the length of vector $\vec{BC}$ as 1. Then the estimated length of vector $\vec{BE}$ is $\frac{1}{\sin{\alpha}}$ So if $\frac{1}{\sin{\alpha}}$ is bigger than the length of half of any adjusted edges, we could calculate a new scaling factor with value $$\pm 0.5 \cdot\min(vec1\textunderscore{}len, vec2\textunderscore{}len))$$ # Additional notes ## Artifacts There are still artifacts in rendering ![obraz](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/416df018-f293-425c-bd38-916ba8137934) ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/7268/reactions","total_count":4,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":4,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/7268/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/comments/2525178176","html_url":"https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/7268#issuecomment-2525178176","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/7268","id":2525178176,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCJ0aI86WgylA","user":{"login":"jni","id":492549,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ5MjU0OQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/492549?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni","html_url":"https://github.com/jni","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jni/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-07T14:07:21Z","updated_at":"2024-12-07T14:07:21Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"Just a quick update here: I've been working my way through a review and tweaking things as I go.
